item.description: President Donald Trump told reporters the Iranian response to a U.S. peace proposal was “stupid” and that the ceasefire is “on massive life support.” NBC News' Jon Allen reports on the war's impasse and how Trump responded to questions over his meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping later in the week.

media:content.media:title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran is 'on massive life support'
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump told reporters the Iranian response to a U.S. peace proposal was “stupid” and that the ceasefire is “on massive life support.” NBC News' Jon Allen reports on the war's impasse and how Trump responded to questions over his meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping later in the week.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran is 'on massive life support'

item.description: Amid a global energy crisis, President Trump says he will suspend the federal gas tax, a move that would require an act of Congress, as more Americans are feeling the pain at the pump. NBC News’ Ryan Nobles reports.

media:content.media:title: President Trump looks to suspend the federal gas tax
media:content.media:description: Amid a global energy crisis, President Trump says he will suspend the federal gas tax, a move that would require an act of Congress, as more Americans are feeling the pain at the pump. NBC News’ Ryan Nobles reports.

item.title: President Trump looks to suspend the federal gas tax

item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley
item.description: The decision sends litigation back to a lower court as the state races to adopt a congressional map that would eliminate a majority-Black district and more heavily favor Republicans.

media:content.media:text: People hold signs outside against redistricting, slogans such as "It's About Us" / "No to Election Rigging" / "It is time we the people say no" / and "Who invited Jim Crow?" can be seen written on signage
media:content.media:description: Several pro-democracy groups hold a rally and a news conference at the Alabama State House in Montgomery on May 4 in response to the special session looking into redrawing congressional maps.

item.title: Supreme Court lets Alabama speed adoption of congressional map eliminating a majority-Black district

item.dc:creator: Shannon Pettypiece
item.description: Under President Donald Trump’s “big, beautiful bill,” food stamp applicants are fighting to prove eligibility. A crisis in Arizona offers a warning for America.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Tiffany Hudson and son Jamie Hudson at their residence. Phoenix on April 28th, 2026.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: People wait in line at St. Mary’s Food Bank
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: SNAP in Arizona
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Dawn Savinski, 62, checked in with a volunteer at St. Mary’s Food Bank.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Enoch Asmah, 41, of Surprise, started volunteering at St. Mary’s Food Bank after coming to the U.S. from Ghana.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Arizona SNAP
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: A volunteers at St. Mary’s Food Bank in Phoenix directs traffic the morning of April 29.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: There was a steady stream of cars throughout the morning of April 29 at St. Mary's Food Bank in Phoenix.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Jena Hannon was among the more than 1,000 people who came to St. Mary’s Food Bank in Phoenix on April 29.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: The Arizona Department of Economic Security location in Peoria had a line of more than 30 people outside its doors when it opened April 29.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Volunteers loaded food into one of the waiting cars at St. Mary’s Food Bank in Phoenix on April 29.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Volunteers filled grocery carts with food to load into vehicles at St. Mary’s Food Bank in Phoenix.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

item.title: Hungry children, canceled benefits: Arizona’s food stamp cuts are a warning for America

item.description: The 18 Americans evacuated from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrived at medical facilities in Omaha and Atlanta. One passenger who tested mildly positive and another showing symptoms were placed in isolation. NBC News’ Camila Bernal reports.

media:content.media:title: Inside American passengers’ journey home from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
media:content.media:description: The 18 Americans evacuated from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrived at medical facilities in Omaha and Atlanta. One passenger who tested mildly positive and another showing symptoms were placed in isolation. NBC News’ Camila Bernal reports.

item.title: Inside American passengers’ journey home from hantavirus-hit cruise ship

item.dc:creator: David Ingram
item.description: Meta, the parent of Facebook and Instagram, is failing to stop scammers who repeatedly buy ads on the platforms to target seniors with scams, a new report says.

media:content.media:text: In scam ads viewed by the researchers, viewers were promised free money via Medicare.
media:content.media:description: In scam ads viewed by the researchers, viewers were promised free money via Medicare.

item.title: Fake Trump and Oprah ads fuel a wave of Medicare scams on Facebook, report says

media:content.media:description: Massive fire breaks out at North Dakota recycling plant

item.title: Massive fire breaks out at North Dakota recycling plant

item.description: Hall of Famer and NBC WNBA Analyst Sue Bird joins "Here's the Scoop" to talk about the fight for fair pay as the league kicks off its 30th season.

media:content.media:title: How pay in the WNBA has changed after 30 seasons
media:content.media:description: Hall of Famer and NBC WNBA Analyst Sue Bird joins "Here's the Scoop" to talk about the fight for fair pay as the league kicks off its 30th season.

item.title: How pay in the WNBA has changed after 30 seasons

item.description: Americans from the hantavirus cruise ship are being assessed and monitored at the National Quarantine Unit in Nebraska. NBC News' Camila Bernal reports on where the passengers are staying.

media:content.media:title: Where hantavirus cruise ship passengers are staying in Nebraska
media:content.media:description: Americans from the hantavirus cruise ship are being assessed and monitored at the National Quarantine Unit in Nebraska. NBC News' Camila Bernal reports on where the passengers are staying.

item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive order challenges the long-standing interpretation of birthright citizenship, arguing it should not apply to children of undocumented immigrants. NBC News’ Laura Jarrett speaks with Lucy Salyer, history professor at University of New Hampshire along with a descendant of Wong Kim Ark — the man behind the landmark 1898 Supreme Court case that solidified birthright citizenship in America — about the broader message it sends if the DOJ succeeds in ending bir

media:content.media:title: Birthright citizenship battle looks to century-old case
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump’s executive order challenges the long-standing interpretation of birthright citizenship, arguing it should not apply to children of undocumented immigrants. NBC News’ Laura Jarrett speaks with Lucy Salyer, history professor at University of New Hampshire along with a descendant of Wong Kim Ark — the man behind the landmark 1898 Supreme Court case that solidified birthright citizenship in America — about the broader message it sends if the DOJ succeeds in ending birthright citizenship.

item.title: Birthright citizenship battle looks to century-old case

item.description: Nike president and CEO Elliott Hill has a schedule filled with meetings, workouts, and — believe it or not — a daily five-minute nap. He sat down with Craig Melvin for a quick look inside his life for NBC News' Business in America.

media:content.media:title: Inside life beyond the company for Nike's CEO
media:content.media:description: Nike president and CEO Elliott Hill has a schedule filled with meetings, workouts, and — believe it or not — a daily five-minute nap. He sat down with Craig Melvin for a quick look inside his life for NBC News' Business in America.

item.title: Inside life beyond the company for Nike's CEO

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts

item.dc:creator: Joe Kottke
Dennis Romero
item.description: Twenty-seven cruise ship workers, including some from the Disney Cruise Line, were deported after having engaged with child pornography, U.S.

media:content.media:text: Disney Magic Cruise Ship in New York City
media:content.media:description: The Disney Magic cruise ship sails in the Hudson River in 2022.

item.title: Investigation found 27 cruise ship workers engaged with child pornography, CBP says

item.dc:creator: David Ingram
item.description: Meta, the parent of Facebook and Instagram, is failing to stop scammers who repeatedly buy ads on the platforms to target seniors with scams, a new report says.

media:content.media:text: In scam ads viewed by the researchers, viewers were promised free money via Medicare.
media:content.media:description: In scam ads viewed by the researchers, viewers were promised free money via Medicare.

item.title: Fake Trump and Oprah ads fuel a wave of Medicare scams on Facebook, report says

item.dc:creator: Maya Rosenberg
Tim Stelloh
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard has taken possession of the sailboat owned by the American man whose wife disappeared in the Bahamas last month, an official said.

media:content.media:text: Brian Hooker.
media:content.media:description: Brian Hooker.

item.title: U.S. Coast Guard now has boat of husband of woman who disappeared in Bahamas
